steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests verb tense consistency. The passage already uses the present tense verb “journey” to describe the butterflies’ yearly migration, and “Each year in the fall” signals a recurring habitual action — so the blank must also be present tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "flew": simple past tense describes a completed past action, which is inconsistent with the present tense “journey” and the recurring yearly context. ✗
  • "were flying": past progressive tense describes an ongoing action at a specific past moment, which is inconsistent with the present tense “journey” used elsewhere in the passage. ✗
  • "had flown": past perfect tense describes an action completed before another past event — neither meaning fits a habitual present-tense migration. ✗
  • "fly": simple present tense is consistent with “journey” and correctly expresses a habitual, yearly occurrence. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
